title: NOC 31200 – Psychologists | GTR Immigration
description: Psychologists are essential mental health professionals supporting individuals, families, schools, and communities across Canada. With increased awareness of mental health, workplace wellness programs, and post-pandemic psychological care needs, demand for qualified psychologists continues to grow nationwide. Classified under NOC 31200 (TEER 1), this occupation qualifies under:
duties: Assess behavior and diagnose emotional, cognitive, and behavioral disorders
Provide individual, family, and group therapy
Conduct psychological testing and standardized assessments
Develop and implement treatment and intervention programs
Support patients managing chronic illness or trauma
Publish research findings and educational materials
Provide consultation services to organizations and government agencies
employment_requirements: Doctoral degree (PhD or PsyD) required to use the title “Psychologist” in:OntarioQuebecManitobaBritish ColumbiaPrince Edward IslandNew Brunswick
Master’s degree sufficient for designation in:AlbertaSaskatchewanNova ScotiaNewfoundland and LabradorNorthwest TerritoriesNunavut
Passing the Examination for Professional Practice in Psychology (EPPP) (required in most provinces)
Oral examinations or board interviews (in some provinces)
Registration with a provincial regulatory body (mandatory nationwide)
Ethics and jurisprudence exams in certain jurisdictions
Without provincial registration, independent practice is not permitted.
job_outlook: This suggests a balanced but steady labor market, with stronger opportunities in:

pathways: Growing public investment in mental health services
Opportunities in both public and private practice
Strong demand in rural and underserved communities
Access to Canada’s public healthcare system
additional_information: HospitalsMental health facilitiesRehabilitation centersCorrectional facilitiesCommunity service organizationsSchools and universitiesPrivate clinicsGovernment and research institutions
Typical annual earnings: $46,200–$113,000 CAD, depending on province, specialization, and years of experience.
Increased demand is particularly noticeable in:
Clinical psychologyChild and school psychologyNeuropsychologyWorkplace and organizational psychologyTrauma and addiction services
